task_objective: Show the per-game disabled notice when exiting a disabled game, not only when starting one, reversing the earlier start-only decision.
tests_added: Inverted the existing exit assertion from an explicit hide to a published game_sync_disabled status, and extended it to advance past RESULT_HIDE_DELAY_MS so the exit notice is proven to auto-hide. Red was confirmed before the implementation edit.
design_decisions: Only the surface complete() mapping changed. Both check_game_start and check_game_exit already returned the same skip reason, and evaluateExitCheck already forwarded non-silent skips to completeStatus, so no backend or decision-layer change was required.

The exit handler's suppression of the pre-check 'checking' publish is retained: nothing is meaningfully verified for a disabled game, so the strip shows the disabled notice directly instead of flashing VERIFYING GAME SAVE first.

Auto-hide coverage was deliberately added to the exit path. A lingering exit strip was a real defect found during review of the original feature; publishing on exit re-enters that code path, so the regression guard is now explicit rather than implied.

results.automated: Targeted suite green (28 tests), then full quality gates: pnpm test, pnpm run build, ruff check/format, ty, pytest.
results.on_device: Deferred and not claimed as passed. Deck verification should confirm the notice appears on quitting a disabled game and clears on its own, with no lingering strip.
results.known_issue_not_addressed: A separate pre-existing defect was diagnosed during this session and deliberately left untouched: settings mutation responses call applySettings wholesale, which overwrites the displayed selected game because ludusaviState conflates ephemeral view selection with the persisted preference. That fix is being planned separately.
